title: scenario_fire_lithium_ion_battery
description: Lithium-ion battery fire in a residential home with thermal runaway risk

timeline.title: Dispatch
timeline.description: Report of fire from an e-bike battery charging in a garage. Occupants evacuated.

timeline.title: En Route
timeline.description: Smoke from garage. Caller confirms e-bike battery is on fire and making popping sounds.

timeline.title: On Scene
timeline.description: Garage with active lithium-ion battery fire. Bright white flames, popping, and smoke. Fire has not extended to the home yet.

timeline.title: Thermal Runaway
timeline.description: Battery cells continuing to vent and reignite. Standard extinguishment not working. Extension to garage storage rack beginning.

timeline.title: Cooling Applied
timeline.description: Large volumes of water applied continuously to cool the battery pack and prevent further thermal runaway.

timeline.title: Scene Secured
timeline.description: Battery cooled. Fire out. Batteries placed in isolation container. Scene secured for monitoring.

node-1.title: Lithium-Ion Fire Recognition
node-1.situation: You arrive to an e-bike battery fire in an attached garage. The battery is venting white smoke and making popping sounds — classic signs of thermal runaway. Bright white flames are present. The fire has not extended to the main structure yet. Occupants are safely outside.
node-1.question: What is your primary suppression approach for this lithium-ion fire?

options.label: A
options.text: Apply large volumes of water continuously to cool the battery cells — water is the correct agent for lithium-ion fires to manage thermal runaway, cool cells, and prevent reignition.

options.label: B
options.text: Use a CO2 extinguisher to smother the fire since it leaves no residue and works on electrical fires.

options.label: C
options.text: Use dry chemical ABC extinguisher since it is rated for all fire classes including electrical.

node-2.title: Managing Reignition Risk
node-2.situation: The visible flames are knocked down with water but the battery pack is continuing to vent smoke and heat. Lithium-ion batteries can reignite minutes or hours later as individual cells reach critical temperature. You cannot simply declare the fire out.
node-2.question: How do you manage the reignition risk?

options.label: A
options.text: Continue applying water to maintain cooling of the battery pack. Do not stop water application prematurely. Plan to isolate the battery in a metal container filled with water for transport to a safe location.

options.label: B
options.text: The fire appears out — stop water application and begin overhaul of the garage.

options.label: C
options.text: Remove the battery from the premises immediately by hand and place it in the yard away from structures.

node-3.title: Isolation and Monitoring
node-3.situation: The battery has been cooled with continuous water application. You need to safely isolate it for monitoring. The battery cannot be left unattended due to reignition risk. You have a metal salvage drum available.
node-3.question: What is your battery isolation procedure?

options.label: A
options.text: Use non-conductive tools to move the battery into the metal drum filled with water. Seal loosely to allow venting. Arrange for extended monitoring or specialized disposal — do not leave the scene until this is complete.

options.label: B
options.text: Leave the battery in the garage with windows open for ventilation and check back in an hour.

options.label: C
options.text: Place the battery in a plastic bin filled with sand to absorb any remaining heat.

node-4.title: Scenario Complete
node-4.situation: The e-bike battery was successfully isolated in a water-filled metal drum with a loose lid for venting. The fire department arranged for hazardous battery disposal by a certified vendor. The garage sustained minor damage. The home was protected. Crew PPE including SCBA was used throughout due to toxic battery off-gas. Well done — correct water application, reignition management, and proper isolation procedures were all executed correctly.
node-4.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
